content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
January 22, 1988 
P. N. Tilden 
Americana Professional Services, Inc. 
Holistic Health Center 
225 South Riverside Avenue 
Rialto, CA 
92376 
Dear Mr. Tilden: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-81-027 
You have asked for advice concerning a mailing you received 
from the Rialto Entertainment Center. 
The Political Reform 
Act, which the Commission administers, does include various 
provisions governing the activities of political committees. 
It appears that the Rialto Entertainment Center is a business 
enterprise, not a political committee. 
Therefore, the 
provisions of the Political Reform Act do not appear to apply. 
DMG:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 T Street. Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box fl07 • 
S:lcr:lfnpnto C'A Qc;R{)d.~{)R{)7 • 
(Qlh)~??_C:;hh('\ 


P. N. Tilden, Th.M., Ph.D. 
M.F.C.C. License #5186 
Licensed Hypnotherapist 
Owner-Director 
Marriage-Family-Child Counselor 
Dale Hickey, M.A. 
Educational Therapist 
Paul Dempsey, Ph.D. Cando 
Marriage-Family-Child Counselor 
Barbara Pearse, M.A. 
M.F.C.C. License #13477 
Bio-Feedback Specialist 
Eileen Thoren, M.A. 
Educ. Psychologist License #187 
Testing Specialist 
Doris Tilden, M.R.E. 
Nutrition & Cosmetics Counselor 
B.J. Shinkunas 
Exercise Counselor 
Georgia Cannon 
Tutor 
AMERICANA PROFESSIONAL SERVICES, INC. 
Holistic Health Center 
225 South Riverside Avenue 
Rialto, California 92376 
January 5, 1988 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Sirs: 
Phone (714) 875-0187 
(714) 820-7617 
I understand that if an organization spends $500 or 
more, or if they mail 200 or more mailings, they must 
file with your office. 
We are organizing a committee 
this week, and will file just as soon as we ralse the 
initial $500. 
Do I understand correctly that such filings are then 
public knowledge? 
If so, please send me the name of the 
organization, and the officers of the same, that sent out 
the very expensive, four-color brochure (copy enclosed) 
to the residents of Rialto. 
The address given on the 
b~chure is just an answering service, and will not give 
us any information. 
Thanks for your help. 
Sincerely, 
P.N. Tilden 
MEMBER: CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF MARRIAGE & FAMILY THERAPISTS 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
January 12, 1988 
P. N. Tilden 
Americana Professional Services, Inc. 
Holistic Health Center 
225 South Riverside Avenue 
Rialto, CA 
92376 
Dear Mr. Tilden: 
Re: 
88-027 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on January 7, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, we will contact you 
shortly to advise you as to the information needed. 
If your 
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as 
quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
~\-

sections.question: I understand that if an organization spends $500 or more, or if they mail 200 or more mailings, they must file with your office. We are organizing a committee this week, and will file just as soon as we raise the initial $500. Do I understand correctly that such filings are then public knowledge? If so, please send me the name of the organization, and the officers of the same, that sent out the very expensive, four-color brochure (copy enclosed) to the residents of Rialto.
sections.conclusion: It appears that the Rialto Entertainment Center is a business enterprise, not a political committee. Therefore, the provisions of the Political Reform Act do not appear to apply.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether organizations that spend $500 or mail 200+ pieces must file with the FPPC and whether such filings are public, and requests the name and officers of the organization that mailed a four-color brochure to Rialto residents.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the Rialto Entertainment Center appears to be a business enterprise rather than a political committee, and therefore the Political Reform Act's provisions do not apply to it.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (dated January 5, 1988) and the FPPC's advice response (dated January 22, 1988). An intermediate acknowledgment letter dated January 12, 1988 is also included. The primary advice letter is the final response from Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel. File number is I-81-027.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I understand that if an organization spends $500 or more, or if they mail 200 or more mailings, they must file with your office. We are organizing a committee this week, and will file just as soon as we raise the initial $500. Do I understand correctly that such filings are then public knowledge? If so, please send me the name of the organization, and the officers of the same, that sent out the very expensive, four-color brochure (copy enclosed) to the residents of Rialto.

CONCLUSION: It appears that the Rialto Entertainment Center is a business enterprise, not a political committee. Therefore, the provisions of the Political Reform Act do not appear to apply.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Rialto Entertainment Center, which sent out a mailing to residents, appears to be a business enterprise rather than a political committee and thus is not subject to the Political Reform Act filing requirements.
